title: atractylis glomerata
intro: Atractylis glomerata, also known as Chinese thistle or St. Barnaby's thistle, is a type of flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to China and other parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. Atractylis glomerata has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and its health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce pain and inflammation. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions, but it should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential side effects.
